title: One Sheet Plywood Console
summary: Build this stylish console table out of a single sheet of plywood!
description: Build this stylish console table out of a single sheet of plywood! Its minimalist yet chic design makes it a versatile piece that will look stunning anywhere in your home.

steps.title: Build the Two Sides
steps.body: Measure, mark, and cut side legs and short aprons to size. Drill 3/4-inch pocket holes into the ends of the short aprons and attach to the side legs with 1 1/4-inch pocket screws. Be sure to drill 3/4-inch pocket holes facing up in the side legs to attach the top in later steps.

steps.title: Add the Front and Back Legs
steps.body: Measure, mark, and cut the front and back legs to size. Attach the front and back legs to the side legs with 1 1/4-inch brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Attach the Bottom Shelf
steps.body: Measure, mark, and cut bottom shelf to size. Drill 3/4-inch pocket holes into the ends and attach to the sides with 1 1/4-inch pocket screws.

steps.title: Add the Long Aprons To the Bottom
steps.body: Measure, mark, and cut long aprons to size. Attach to carcass as shown with 1 1/4-inch brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Add the Long Aprons To the Top
steps.body: Measure, mark, and cut long aprons to size. Drill 3/4-inch pocket holes into the ends and attach to carcass with 1 1/4-inch pocket screws. Be sure to drill 3/4-inch pocket holes facing up to attach the top in later steps.

steps.title: Attach the Middle Legs
steps.body: Measure, mark, and cut middle legs to size. Drill 3/4-inch pocket holes into the ends, find the midpoint in the carcass and attach middle legs with 1 1/4-inch pocket screws.

steps.title: Add the Curved Details
steps.body: Measure, mark, and cut curved pieces out with a jigsaw, bandsaw, or scroll saw. Attach as shown with wood glue and 1 inch brad nails.

steps.title: Add the Underside Support
steps.body: Measure, mark, and cut middle support to size. Drill 3/4-inch pocket holes into ends and attach as shown with 1 1/4-inch pocket screws.

steps.title: Attach the Top
steps.body: Measure, mark, and cut top to size. Position over the carcass with a 1/2-inch overhang on all sides. Attach with 1 1/4-inch pocket screws driven from the aprons underneath.

steps.title: Visit JenWoodhouse.com
steps.body: Visit JenWoodhouse.com for more information, DIY project ideas, and building plans.
